patches.rationale: WIN-017.c3 H0: TLDR factual error. The argument is that parallax scales with 1/distance; a firmament wobble would produce the same angular displacement regardless of target DISTANCE, not brightness. Single-word fix.

patches.find: the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call, making even that weaker test unverifiable
patches.replace: there is no live fetch call here at all — WIN-017 appears only as a static entry in the dome WIN registry — so even the nominal test that would check the hardcoded value against a live fetch is absent
patches.rationale: WIN-017.c3 H1+H4: Code analysis tag changes to monitoring=none (no monitor.py entry for WIN-017). The old boilerplate said hardcoded+live-fetch; correct none-monitoring boilerplate mirrors WIN-016.

patches.find: there is no meaningful sense in which the dome monitor script "predicted" it before reading the data.
patches.replace: there is no meaningful sense in which the dome monitor script "predicted" it before reading the data. Nor is the 20 m wobble amplitude derived — it is the value required to produce arctan(amp/H) ≈ 0.454 arcsec at H=9,086 km, fitted backward from the textbook range.
patches.rationale: WIN-017.c3 H4: detail_verdict_text omits that the dome-specific 20 m wobble amplitude is also reverse-engineered (not derived from dome geometry). Adding this closes the post-hoc framing gap.

patches.find: The textbook value was publicly known.
patches.replace: The 0-0.5 arcsec parallax range is textbook physics; the 20 m wobble amplitude is reverse-engineered to match it.
patches.rationale: WIN-017.c3 H5: tldr_verdict second sentence was vague ('textbook value was publicly known') without specifying which value. Replacement names both the range and the fitted parameter, giving the reader the punchline.
